{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/250794953?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/yuki-matsuhashi", "id": 250794953, "login": "yuki-matsuhashi", "node_id": "U_kgDODvLTyQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/yuki-matsuhashi", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-8813", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:P"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-1284", "name": "Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input"}], "description": "### Impact

When parsing an image with an embedded ICC profile that contains a crafted `multiLocalizedUnicodeType` (`mluc`) tag, ExifReader can be made to allocate memory proportional to attacker-controlled fields in the tag rather than to
the actual size of the input. Processing such an image causes excessive memory consumption and can terminate the host process (out-of-memory).

Any application that calls `ExifReader.load()` on untrusted images, for example, user uploads in a web service, is affected. ICC profiles are carried in JPEG, TIFF, PNG, HEIC, AVIF, JPEG XL, and WebP, so the issue is reachable from any of those formats.

### Patches

Fixed in `exifreader@4.39.0`. Upgrade with:

    npm install exifreader@latest

Bower users consume the bundled `dist/` files from this repository, and the same fix is committed there.

### Workarounds

If upgrading is not immediately possible, configure a [custom build](https://github.com/mattiasw/ExifReader#configure-a-custom-build) that excludes the `icc` module so that ICC parsing (and therefore this code path) is skipped entirely.

### Resources

- Patch: https://github.com/mattiasw/ExifReader/commit/c9d88b67e127b2dcc7b46e328df468257fb2dc30", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00528, "percentile": 0.40528}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-29T17:58:37Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-8813"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-19T07:16:30Z", "published_at": "2026-05-29T17:58:37Z", "references": ["https://github.com/mattiasw/ExifReader/security/advisories/GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-8813", "https://github.com/mattiasw/ExifReader/commit/c9d88b67e127b2dcc7b46e328df468257fb2dc30", "https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-EXIFREADER-16689335", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/mattiasw/ExifReader/security-advisories/GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/mattiasw/ExifReader", "summary": "ExifReader is vulnerable to denial of service via crafted ICC `mluc` tag", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-17T16:51:54Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-h64w-w9pr-82m4", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.39.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "exifreader"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.10.0, < 4.39.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: ExifReader is vulnerable to denial of service via crafted ICC `mluc` tag
